name: cliff-jumper
description: A small CLI tool to create a semantic release and git-cliff powered Changelog

name: --package-path
-p
options.description: The path to the current package. For non-monorepos this is just "."

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Whether the package should be bumped or not. When this is set no actions will be taken and only the release strategy will be logged

options.name: --first-release
options.description: Whether this is the first release (skips bumping the version)'

options.name: --mono-repo
options.description:
Whether the package to be bumped resides in a mono repo,
which enables Lerna-like scanning for what kind of version bump should be applied
Defaults to "true" when "org" is set, false otherwise

options.name: --no-mono-repo
options.description:
Whether the package to be bumped resides in a mono repo,
which enables Lerna-like scanning for what kind of version bump should be applied
Defaults to "true" when "org" is set, false otherwise

name: --org
-o
options.description: The NPM org scope that should be used. This should be WITHOUT "@" sign or trailing "/"

options.name: --preid
options.description: The "prerelease identifier" to use as a prefix for the "prerelease" part of a semver

name: --commit-message-template
-c
options.description:
A custom commit message template to use.
Defaults to "chore({{name}}): release {{full-name}}@{{new-version}}"
You can use "{{new-version}}" in your template which will be dynamically replaced with whatever the new version is that will be published.
You can use "{{name}}" in your template, this will be replaced with the name provided through "-n", "--name" or the same value set in your config file.
You can use "{{full-name}}" in your template, this will be replaced "{{name}}" (when "org" is not provided), or "@{{org}}/{{name}}" (when "org" is provided).

options.name: --tag-template
options.description:
A custom tag template to use.
When "org" is provided this will default to "@{{org}}/{{name}}@{{new-version}}", for example "@favware/cliff-jumper@1.0.0"
When "org" is not provided this will default to "v{{new-version}}", for example "v1.0.0"
You can use "{{new-version}}" in your template which will be dynamically replaced with whatever the new version is that will be published.
You can use "{{org}}" in your template, this will be replaced with the org provided through "-o", "--org" or the same value set in your config file.
You can use "{{name}}" in your template, this will be replaced with the name provided through "-n", "--name" or the same value set in your config file.
You can use "{{full-name}}" in your template, this will be replaced "{{name}}" (when "org" is not provided), or "@{{org}}/{{name}}" (when "org" is provided).

name: --install
-i
options.description: Whether to run npm install (or your package manager's equivalent) after bumping the version but before committing and creating a git tag. This is useful when you have a mono repo where bumping one package would then cause the lockfile to be out of date

options.name: --skip-changelog
options.description:
Whether to skip updating your CHANGELOG.md
default "true" when CI=true, "false" otherwise

options.name: --no-skip-changelog
options.description:
Whether to skip updating your CHANGELOG.md
default "true" when CI=true, "false" otherwise

name: --skip-tag
-t
options.description:
Whether to skip creating a git tag
default "true" when CI=true, "false" otherwise

options.name: --no-skip-tag
options.description:
Whether to skip creating a git tag
default "true" when CI=true, "false" otherwise
